comments.author: @RealWorldExplorer-k7z
comments.text: Ireland is so much more than green hills and pubs. This documentary really opened my eyes

comments.author: @globerealife
comments.text: With over 36 million Americans tracing their ancestry back to this tiny, rain-soaked island, the connection between our two nations runs incredibly deep. Have any of you successfully traced your family's journey back to the specific Irish parish they left during the Great Hunger?

comments.author: @RealLifeDocumentaries.EN26
comments.text: To anyone watching this and thinking of visiting Ireland: DO IT. But bring a good raincoat... and be ready for the best pint of Guinness of your life.

comments.author: @AmazingDiscoveriesTopUS
comments.text: Ireland has 5 million people... but 70 million claim Irish roots. Is that the world's strongest diaspora?

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: Captivating! Those fierce Irish women and the ancient roots of Hurling show the true soul of Ireland.

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: It’s incredibly sad to see the "Leprechaun Economics" paradox [25:04]. Ireland is technically one of the richest countries in Europe on paper (housing tech giants like Google and Apple), yet they are facing a brutal housing crisis where locals can't even afford rent in Dublin [25:24]. For the Irish watching this: is the tech boom actually destroying the local culture, or is it a necessary evil? Let me know! 👇

replies.author: @bubbahix8933
replies.text: Immigration is the cause of Irelands housing problem

comments.author: @LennoxCarew
comments.text: I'm viewing from Trinidad and Tobago.  83 years old with Irish blood and proud of it also Thank you

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: Irish women with natural red hair and green eyes are undeniably striking, but their real charm is that classic Irish wit, humor, and sharp intellect. This documentary beautifully captures the warmth of the people. There is an old saying: 'There are no strangers here; only friends you haven't yet met.' This video proved exactly that inside every local pub!
